<!-- GENERATED by `bun run gen` from scenarios/02-isolation/lost-update-repeatable-read.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

*The same two +10 deposits — but this time at REPEATABLE READ.*

```
A> BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;
BEGIN

A> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)

B> BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;
BEGIN

B> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)

A> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 110 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE 1

A> COMMIT;
COMMIT
```

*B's snapshot predates A's commit, so B's write is refused instead of silently clobbering it.*

```
B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 110 WHERE id = 1;
ERROR:  40001: could not serialize access due to concurrent update

B> ROLLBACK;
ROLLBACK

A> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     110 
(1 row)
```

*Retrying B from scratch reads the fresh 110 and correctly produces 120.*

<small>Verified against PostgreSQL 18.4 · [Run it yourself](/about/run-locally) · [Scenario source](https://github.com/svyatov/postgres-transactions/blob/main/scenarios/02-isolation/lost-update-repeatable-read.ts)</small>
